openmeter/ent.purpose: Organisational root for Ent ORM code: openmeter/ent/schema owns every PostgreSQL table/view definition (source of truth), openmeter/ent/tx bridges Ent transactions to the framework transaction.Creator interface, and the root-level entc.go drives `go generate` to emit all code into openmeter/ent/db/.

patterns.name: Schema-first generation
patterns.description: All database shapes live in openmeter/ent/schema/*.go. Run `make generate` after any schema change; never edit openmeter/ent/db/.
patterns.example: go run -mod=readonly entc.go  // triggered by go:generate in generate.go

patterns.name: Extension-driven codegen
patterns.description: entc.go registers five custom extensions (entcursor, entexpose, entmixinaccessor, entpaginate, entsetorclear) plus FeatureVersionedMigration, FeatureLock, FeatureUpsert, FeatureExecQuery. Any new Ent feature must be added here, not inline.

patterns.name: View SQL side-output
patterns.description: entc.go calls viewgen.GenerateFile after Ent codegen to produce tools/migrate/views.sql. View DDL is not in atlas migrations — it is regenerated separately.

patterns.name: Transaction bridging via openmeter/ent/tx
patterns.description: Domain adapters must obtain transactions through the transaction.Creator interface (implemented in openmeter/ent/tx) rather than calling db.Tx(ctx) directly, so pkg/framework/entutils.TransactingRepo can rebind the ctx-carried transaction.
patterns.example: enttx.New(db) // returns transaction.Creator backed by db.HijackTx

key_file_guides.role: Single entry point for `go generate ./...` in this package; configures all Ent features and extensions, sets output target to ./db, and emits views.sql.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new Ent feature flag or extension — must be added here; changes take effect only after `make generate`.

key_file_guides.role: Contains the `//go:generate go run -mod=readonly entc.go` directive consumed by `make generate`.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add logic here; it is a one-liner trigger file.

key_file_guides.role: Sole file in the tx sub-package; implements transaction.Creator via db.HijackTx so entutils.TransactingRepo can bind adapters to ctx-carried transactions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add business logic; do not construct transactions elsewhere (e.g. db.Tx(ctx) in an adapter).

anti_patterns: Editing any file under openmeter/ent/db/ — fully generated, overwritten by make generate.
Calling db.Tx(ctx) directly in an adapter instead of going through transaction.Creator (openmeter/ent/tx) — bypasses entutils.TransactingRepo transaction rebinding.
Adding Ent feature flags or extensions outside entc.go — they will be silently absent from generated code.
Committing views.sql edits manually — it is regenerated by entc.go's viewgen call on every make generate run.
Skipping `make generate` after schema changes — openmeter/ent/db/ will be stale and builds will fail or behave incorrectly.

decisions.decision: entc.go as the single codegen driver rather than per-schema generators
decisions.rationale: Centralises feature flags and extensions so all five custom extensions (cursor, expose, mixin accessor, paginate, set-or-clear) are applied uniformly to every entity.

decisions.decision: Separate openmeter/ent/tx package for transaction bridging
decisions.rationale: Keeps the transaction.Creator abstraction out of the generated db package, preventing import cycles between generated code and pkg/framework.

openmeter/event.purpose: Organisational namespace for cross-subsystem event primitives. openmeter/event/metadata owns canonical event naming (io.openmeter.<subsystem>.<version>.<name>), resource path construction (//openmeter.io/...), and entity type constants. openmeter/event/models holds minimal shared payload value types (FeatureKeyAndID, NamespaceID) used in event payloads across domain boundaries.

patterns.name: EventType triple for naming
patterns.description: All event names are constructed via metadata.EventType{Subsystem, Version, Name} and metadata.GetEventName(); never build io.openmeter.* strings manually.

patterns.name: Resource path construction
patterns.description: All //openmeter.io/... URIs must be built with metadata.ComposeResourcePath(namespace, entityConst, id); never use fmt.Sprintf.

patterns.name: Entity constant registry in metadata
patterns.description: New entity types must be added as EntityXxx constants in openmeter/event/metadata, not inlined as strings in event structs.

patterns.name: Inline Validate() on every model
patterns.description: Every type in openmeter/event/models must implement Validate() — called at event deserialization boundaries to catch missing required fields.
patterns.example: func (m NamespaceID) Validate() error { if m.ID == "" { return errors.New(...) } }

key_file_guides.role: Defines EventType, EventSubsystem, EventName, EventMetadata, and GetEventName — the canonical naming contract for all events.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding new subsystems must follow the io.openmeter.<subsystem>.<version>.<name> pattern; version must be explicit (e.g. 'v1').

key_file_guides.role: Defines ComposeResourcePath and all EntityXxx constants for constructing //openmeter.io/... URIs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New domain entities must register an EntityXxx constant here before being used in event source/subject fields.

key_file_guides.role: Holds cross-subsystem minimal payload types (FeatureKeyAndID, NamespaceID). Must not import domain packages to avoid circular dependencies.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add domain-specific rich types (Invoice, Subscription) here — they belong in their own domain packages.

anti_patterns: Building io.openmeter.* event name strings with string concatenation instead of EventType.EventName().
Constructing //openmeter.io/... resource paths with fmt.Sprintf instead of ComposeResourcePath.
Adding a new entity type as an inline string literal in an event struct instead of an EntityXxx constant in metadata.
Importing openmeter domain packages from openmeter/event/models — creates circular dependencies.
Skipping Validate() calls on values received in event payloads — missing required fields go undetected.

decisions.rationale: metadata owns naming/routing primitives while models holds cross-domain payload types; keeping them separate prevents the naming package from accumulating domain-specific types.

openmeter/info.purpose: Organisational namespace for the info domain. Currently contains only openmeter/info/httpdriver, which exposes stateless endpoints (e.g. currency list) via the httptransport pattern with no domain service dependency — data is sourced directly from external libraries (gobl/currency) or static definitions.

patterns.name: Dependency-free stateless handlers
patterns.description: When an endpoint's data comes from a static library rather than a DB-backed service, the handler struct holds no injected service. Avoid adding service fields to keep these handlers trivially testable.
patterns.example: type handler struct{} // no fields; operation func calls gobl/currency.All() directly

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandler triple
patterns.description: Every endpoint follows the decoder/operation/encoder triple: decoder extracts HTTP params, operation is pure business logic, encoder serialises the response.

patterns.name: No-op request decoder for parameterless endpoints
patterns.description: Endpoints with no parameters use a decoder that returns an empty struct and nil error rather than reading from the request.

patterns.name: Handler interface + private handler struct
patterns.description: driver.go declares the public Handler interface; per-resource files (like currencies.go) implement a private handler struct with a New constructor.

key_file_guides.role: Public Handler interface declaration and New constructor — the only symbols exported to callers (router.go).
key_file_guides.watch_for: New endpoints must be added to the Handler interface here before being implemented in a per-resource file.

key_file_guides.role: ListCurrencies endpoint implementation — canonical example of a no-service stateless handler sourcing data from gobl/currency.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not inject a service or adapter; if new data requires DB access it belongs in a new domain package, not here.

anti_patterns: Injecting a domain service or adapter into a handler struct when data comes from a static library.
Returning a raw error from the operation func instead of a typed domain error (models.GenericValidationError, etc.) — breaks the generic error encoder chain.
Hand-editing generated API types in api/ instead of regenerating via make gen-api.
Adding request validation logic inside the decoder func instead of returning a validation error from the operation func.

decisions.decision: No domain service or Wire provider needed for info/httpdriver
decisions.rationale: All current endpoints are backed by static library calls; adding a Wire provider for purely static data would add unnecessary DI boilerplate.

openmeter/llmcost.purpose: LLM cost price management domain: persists global (synced) prices and per-namespace manual overrides in llmcostprice, resolves effective prices with namespace-override precedence, and synchronises prices from external sources (models.dev). All monetary values use alpacadecimal.Decimal for precision.

patterns.name: Validate() on every input and domain type
patterns.description: All input types implement models.Validator (compile-time asserted with `var _ models.Validator = (*XInput)(nil)`). Call input.Validate() at the service boundary before delegating to the adapter.
patterns.example:
var _ models.Validator = (*ListPricesInput)(nil)
func (i ListPricesInput) Validate() error { ... return models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...)) }

patterns.name: models.NewNillableGenericValidationError wrapping
patterns.description: All Validate() methods return models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...)) — returns nil when errs is empty, ensuring consistent error typing for HTTP encoding.

patterns.name: ValidationIssue sentinel errors for field-level errors
patterns.description: Each error condition has a named sentinel models.NewValidationIssue with ErrCodeXxx constant, field path, severity, and HTTP status attribute. New error conditions follow this pattern in errors.go.
patterns.example: var ErrProviderEmpty = models.NewValidationIssue(ErrCodeProviderEmpty, "provider must not be empty", models.WithFieldString("provider"), models.WithCriticalSeverity(), commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute(http.StatusBadRequest))

patterns.name: PriceSource discrimination for global vs override rows
patterns.description: Rows with namespace IS NULL and source='system' are global prices; rows with namespace IS NOT NULL and source='manual' are overrides. Never mix these in a single query path.

patterns.name: alpacadecimal.Decimal for all price fields
patterns.description: All cost-per-token fields use alpacadecimal.Decimal (never float64 or string). Optional token dimensions (CacheRead, CacheWrite, Reasoning) are *alpacadecimal.Decimal.

patterns.name: NormalizeModelID before any price lookup or insert
patterns.description: Call llmcost.NormalizeModelID(provider, modelID) before storing or resolving prices to canonicalise casing, version suffixes, region prefixes, and provider aliases.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping in adapter
patterns.description: Every adapter method that writes must be wrapped with entutils.TransactingRepo / TransactingRepoWithNoValue so ctx-carried transactions are honored.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, tx *adapter) (Price, error) { ... })

key_file_guides.role: Core domain types: Price, ModelPricing, PriceSource, SourcePrice, SourcePricesMap. All fields validated in Validate() methods.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding new token-cost dimensions requires updating ModelPricing, Validate(), and all mapping/adapter code.

key_file_guides.role: Service interface definition plus all input types with their Validate() methods. Compile-time Validator assertions guard every input type.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New Service methods require a matching XInput type with Validate() and a compile-time assertion here.

key_file_guides.role: Adapter interface declaration (extends entutils.TxCreator). Source of truth for which persistence operations exist.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any new query or write must be added to Adapter before implementing in openmeter/llmcost/adapter/.

key_file_guides.role: All ValidationIssue sentinels and error constructors for the llmcost domain.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New field-level errors must follow the ErrCodeXxx constant + models.NewValidationIssue pattern with explicit HTTP status.

key_file_guides.role: NormalizeModelID and NormalizeProvider: strips version/region suffixes, normalises provider aliases. Must be called before any price store or resolve.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding new provider aliases or version-suffix patterns requires updating this file and its test table.

key_file_guides.role: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter; all writes use TransactingRepo; ResolvePrice uses ORDER BY namespace DESC to prefer overrides.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never call a.db directly outside TransactingRepo in write paths — falls off ctx transaction.

key_file_guides.role: Business-logic layer; applies namespace-override overlay in ListPrices at service layer, not adapter layer; wraps mutations in transaction.Run.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add DB queries inside the overlay loop — batch-fetch overrides once before iterating.

key_file_guides.role: Four-phase sync pipeline: Fetch → Normalize → Deduplicate → Reconcile. Writes via Adapter.UpsertGlobalPrice only after multi-source agreement.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Fetcher errors must not abort the sync for all models; reconciler.Reconcile must follow deduplicateSourcePrices.

anti_patterns: Using float64 or string for price fields — must use alpacadecimal.Decimal to preserve billing precision.
Skipping NormalizeModelID before storing or resolving prices — causes provider/model ID mismatches across sources.
Calling a.db directly in adapter helpers without TransactingRepo — bypasses ctx-carried Ent transaction.
Adding source='manual' rows via UpsertGlobalPrice (reserved for source='system' reconciled prices).
Calling reconciler.Reconcile before deduplicateSourcePrices — allows false multi-source agreement from provider aliasing.

decisions.decision: Namespace-override overlay applied at service layer, not adapter layer
decisions.rationale: Keeps the adapter queries simple (global vs override are separate query paths); the service batches both and merges them in memory.

decisions.decision: Multi-source agreement threshold before writing global prices
decisions.rationale: A single down source should not pollute global prices; requiring minAgreement sources within priceTolerance prevents rogue data from one fetcher.

decisions.decision: Normalisation as a pure package-level function (NormalizeModelID)
decisions.rationale: Applied uniformly by both the sync pipeline and the adapter/service, eliminating per-caller inconsistency in canonical key construction.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new service method with input validation

openmeter/meter.purpose: Meter domain: defines usage aggregation rules (event type, aggregation function, group-by JSON paths), manages meter lifecycle with soft-delete semantics, validates events against meters at ingest time, and publishes Watermill events after mutations. ManageService extends Service with mutation and pre-update hooks consumed by billing and entitlement.

patterns.name: Service/ManageService interface split
patterns.description: Callers that only need reads depend on meter.Service; callers needing mutations depend on meter.ManageService (which embeds Service). Wire providers inject ManageService where mutation is needed.
patterns.example: type ManageService interface { Service; CreateMeter(...); UpdateMeter(...); DeleteMeter(...); RegisterHooks(...) }

patterns.name: Validate() on every input type
patterns.description: All input types (CreateMeterInput, UpdateMeterInput, DeleteMeterInput, GetMeterInput, ListMetersParams) have explicit Validate() methods called before any adapter or service logic runs.

patterns.name: models.GenericError wrapping for domain errors
patterns.description: MeterNotFoundError wraps models.NewGenericNotFoundError and implements models.GenericError. Use IsMeterNotFoundError(err) for type-checked error inspection.

patterns.name: Watermill event publishing after mutations
patterns.description: Service layer publishes domain events (MeterCreateEvent, MeterUpdateEvent, MeterDeleteEvent) after successful adapter writes using metadata.EventType triple and ComposeResourcePath.
patterns.example: NewMeterCreateEvent(ctx, &meter) // implements EventName()+EventMetadata() for Watermill routing

patterns.name: ValueProperty rules enforced on model and input
patterns.description: COUNT aggregation forbids ValueProperty; all others require it with a '$' prefix. GroupBy values must start with '$'; keys must match groupByKeyRegExp. These invariants are checked in both Meter.Validate() and CreateMeterInput.Validate().
patterns.example: if aggregation == MeterAggregationCount && valueProperty != nil { return errors.New(...) }

patterns.name: Soft-delete via DeletedAt
patterns.description: Meters are never hard-deleted. ListMeters excludes soft-deleted meters unless IncludeDeleted=true. DeleteMeter sets DeletedAt; idempotent on already-deleted meters.

patterns.name: ParseEvent for ingest validation
patterns.description: meter.ParseEvent(meter, data) validates and extracts value and group-by fields from CloudEvent JSON. Returns ErrInvalidEvent for bad data, ErrInvalidMeter for configuration errors. COUNT meters get Value=1.0; UNIQUE_COUNT meters get ValueString.
patterns.example: parsedEvent, err := meter.ParseEvent(m, rawData) // returns *ParsedEvent{Value, ValueString, GroupBy}

key_file_guides.role: Service and ManageService interface definitions plus all input types with Validate(). Single source of truth for the meter API surface.
key_file_guides.watch_for: WithoutNamespace flag in ListMetersParams is the only safe way to list meters across namespaces — never pass empty namespace without this flag.

key_file_guides.role: Meter domain type, MeterAggregation/WindowSize enums, aggregation/group-by validation helpers, and MeterQueryRow result type.
key_file_guides.watch_for: validateMeterAggregation and validateMeterGroupBy are shared between Meter.Validate() and CreateMeterInput.Validate() — keep them in sync.

key_file_guides.role: ParseEvent and ParseEventString: validate and extract value/group-by from CloudEvent JSON at ingest time. ErrInvalidEvent = data error, ErrInvalidMeter = config error.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GROUP BY missing fields return empty string (not error) — this is intentional and must not be changed.

key_file_guides.role: Domain event structs (MeterCreateEvent, MeterUpdateEvent, MeterDeleteEvent) implementing EventName()/EventMetadata() for Watermill. Each uses metadata.ComposeResourcePath for source/subject.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MeterDeleteEvent.Validate() requires DeletedAt to be non-nil — always set by the adapter before constructing the event.

key_file_guides.role: MeterNotFoundError wrapping models.GenericNotFoundError and IsMeterNotFoundError helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use IsMeterNotFoundError for type-checked matching rather than errors.As directly on the inner error.

key_file_guides.role: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter; enforces soft-delete, namespace isolation, and TransactingRepo wrapping.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All queries must include DeletedAt IS NULL predicate unless IncludeDeleted is true.

key_file_guides.role: In-memory mock for tests; optionally syncs to PG via SetDBClient for FK constraint satisfaction.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Test-only — never wire mockadapter into production. SetDBClient must be called before meters are seeded to sync to PG.

anti_patterns: Passing empty Namespace to ListMeters without WithoutNamespace=true — silently returns no results or errors.
Using float64 for MeterQueryRow.Value in places where NaN/Inf are possible — explicitly rejected in ParseEvent.
Adding DB queries directly in service/manage.go — all persistence must go through the adapter interface.
Skipping validateJSONPaths (streaming.Connector) before CreateMeter/UpdateMeter — invalid paths fail silently until query time.
Hard-deleting meter rows instead of setting DeletedAt — breaks FK integrity and idempotent delete semantics.

decisions.decision: Service layer publishes Watermill events after adapter mutations rather than in the adapter
decisions.rationale: Keeps adapter purely concerned with persistence; event publishing is a cross-cutting side-effect that belongs at the orchestration layer.

decisions.decision: ManageService holds a direct *adapter.Adapter reference alongside embedded Service
decisions.rationale: Allows ManageService to call adapter methods not exposed on the Service interface (e.g. soft-delete helpers) without bloating Service with write operations.

decisions.decision: mockadapter optional PG sync via SetDBClient rather than always requiring DB
decisions.rationale: Most unit tests need only in-memory meter resolution; tests that create features with meter FK constraints opt-in by calling SetDBClient.

code_examples.scenario: Publishing a domain event after a meter mutation
